key_assumptions.source: Sum of parts / catalyst path in IRR steps
Model choice in valuation.json (not company guidance)

key_assumptions.input: Residual to Lawrence base payoff uplift

open_questions: Price refreshed: Stooq **$55.00** (2026-05-22) vs mgmt repurchase **$27** (Jun 2024)
thesis: Keweenaw Land Association owns mineral rights on more than one million net acres in Michigan's Upper Peninsula. **[Fact]** Revenue today is mineral lease income, primarily from Highland Copper's Copperwood project leases (~$349,700 in FY2024). **[Fact]** The company is not an operator; it collects rent and royalties if lessees develop mines. **[Fact]** Book value was about $14.3 million (~$12.70 per share) at December 2024 with roughly $4 million in cash and treasuries against ~$160,000 per year operating burn. **[Fact]** In June 2024 management repurchased 51,633 shares at $27 and acquired 667,000 additional acres at roughly $1.52 per acre. The stock trades OTC with very thin volume.
why_market_wrong: The market may either ignore Copperwood production royalties priced at zero in lease-only cash flow, or overpay after a move from management's $27 repurchase to much higher OTC quotes. **[Inference]** At **$59.33** (Yahoo close, 2026-07-23), roughly 4.7× book, the dormant asset (Copperwood royalties) is partially in the price. **[Inference]** OTC illiquidity and CEO-prepared statements keep the acreage option opaque to generalists. SSI framing (May 2024) used **$4/lb copper**; we scale the SSI royalty bridge to spot copper when available. Lawrence base still assumes **no production royalties** in operating cash; overlay uses **35% probability** on spot royalty math.
